root_text.sn13.5:1.2: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso mahāpathaviyā satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā upanikkhipeyya.
root_text.sn13.5:1.4: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ, yā vā satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā upanikkhittā yā vā mahāpathavī”ti?
root_text.sn13.5:2.3: Neva satimaṁ kalaṁ upenti na sahassimaṁ kalaṁ upenti na satasahassimaṁ kalaṁ upenti mahāpathaviṁ upanidhāya satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā upanikkhittā”ti.

translation_text.sn13.5:1.2: “Mendicants, suppose a person was to place seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds on the great earth.
translation_text.sn13.5:1.4: Which is more: the seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds, or the great earth?”
translation_text.sn13.5:2.1: “Sir, the great earth is certainly more.
translation_text.sn13.5:2.2: The seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds are tiny.
translation_text.sn13.5:2.3: Compared to the great earth, it’s not nearly a hundredth, a thousandth, or a hundred thousandth part.”
translation_text.sn13.5:2.4: “In the same way, for a noble disciple, the suffering that’s over and done with is more …”
